channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SAD Plans to Screen Historical Film 'Satluj' Across Punjab to Highlight Militancy Era
item.description: The Shiromani Akali Dal (SAD) has announced plans to screen the film 'Satluj', previously titled 'Punjab ’95', across Punjab after its removal from an OTT platform. The movie details the life of activ...

item.title: Typhoon Inday Downgraded to Typhoon; PAGASA Updates on Path and Impact
item.description: Super Typhoon Inday, which entered the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) earlier this week, has been downgraded to a typhoon by the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services ...

item.title: Vintage Portraits of Pier Angeli on the Set of 'Somebody Up There Likes Me' (1956)
item.description: This article explores vintage photographs capturing Italian actress Pier Angeli during the filming of the 1956 boxing drama 'Somebody Up There Likes Me.' The images highlight her delicate beauty and q...

item.title: Top 10 F1 Drivers of the 2000s: A Decade of Dominance and Innovation
item.description: The article ranks the top 10 Formula 1 drivers of the 2000s, highlighting their achievements, impact, and contributions to the sport. It begins by noting the era's transition from V10 engines to newer...

item.title: Teenagers Arrested After Police Find $11K in Counterfeit Cash at Abbotsford Construction Site
item.description: A pair of teenagers were arrested after authorities discovered $11,000 in counterfeit currency at a construction site in Abbotsford. The incident occurred when police responded to the location and fou...

item.title: Trump Discusses Security Precautions and Iranian Threats During Air Force One Press Briefing
item.description: President Donald Trump addressed reporters aboard Air Force One while returning to Washington after the NATO summit in Turkey, responding to questions about unusual security measures during the flight...

item.title: Shift in AI Focus: From Model Development to Industrial Application
item.description: The 2026 AI Engineer World's Fair, held in San Francisco from July 1-4, marked a significant shift in the global AI industry's priorities. While previous editions focused on developing ever-larger mod...

item.title: SM Seaside Cebu Arena to Host 2027 FIBA Women's Asia Cup
item.description: The SM Seaside Cebu Arena has been officially announced as the venue for the 2027 FIBA Women's Asia Cup, a major international basketball tournament set to showcase the Philippines' capabilities in ho...

item.title: Church Offering Dispute Sparks Online Debate Over Dating Etiquette
item.description: A Nigerian woman went viral after criticizing a man she met in church for asking for her phone number without first paying her church offering. The incident, shared in a viral video, sparked heated on...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Suspends Campaign Amid Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate for Maine's U.S. Senate seat, has suspended his campaign following allegations of sexual assault that have led to the withdrawal of endorsements. The candidate...

item.title: Disneyland Halts Tiana's Bayou Ride After Child Attempts to Exit Before 50-Foot Drop
item.description: A child allegedly attempted to exit Disneyland's Tiana's Bayou Adventure ride on Saturday night, prompting an emergency stop of the attraction. This incident marks the second reported case in about a ...

item.title: Alberta Auditor General to Complete Health Contract Probe by Year-End
item.description: Alberta’s new auditor general, Phil Peters, has stated that his investigation into a health-care contracting scandal is expected to be completed by the end of 2026. The probe, which focuses on multimi...

item.title: Escalating US-Iran Tensions as Trump Ends Ceasefire and Threatens Military Action
item.description: US President Donald Trump has declared the ceasefire with Iran over, escalating tensions between the two nations. The conflict, which began in late February with US-Israeli strikes on Iran, has intens...

item.title: Former Fox News Anchor Condemns Democratic Candidate's Denial of Sexual Assault Allegations
item.description: Former Fox News anchor Gretchen Carlson harshly criticized Graham Platner, a Democratic Senate candidate in Maine, for his continued denial of sexual assault allegations as he suspended his campaign. ...

item.title: Review of 'How to Get Filthy Rich With Gary Stevenson': A Disappointing Take on Wealth Tax Proposals
item.description: The article reviews the documentary 'How to Get Filthy Rich With Gary Stevenson', which critiques Gary Stevenson's advocacy for a 2% wealth tax on assets exceeding £10m. Stevenson, a former financial ...

item.title: Tragedy at World Cup screening in Gaza as Israeli strike kills organizer
item.description: An Israeli strike in Gaza has killed a World Cup screening organizer moments before the event's kickoff, marking a tragic intersection of international sports and ongoing conflict. The incident occurr...

item.title: US and Iran Exchange Escalating Strikes Amid Tensions
item.description: The United States and Iran engaged in a renewed cycle of military strikes following tensions over attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump ordered retaliatory strikes agains...

item.title: New Psychiatric Hospital to Boost Mental Healthcare and Create Jobs in Port Shepstone
item.description: A new 28-bed psychiatric hospital, costing R240 million, is set to be built in Port Shepstone, KwaZulu-Natal. The project, spearheaded by the KZN Departments of Health and Public Works, aims to addres...

item.title: Father accused of killing wife and daughters flees to Zimbabwe as police hunt begins
item.description: UK police are actively searching for Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, a 45-year-old British citizen of Zimbabwean heritage, who is suspected of murdering his wife, Zandile Tshuma, 42, and their daughters, Na...

item.title: US removes Syria from terror blacklist, signaling diplomatic shift
item.description: The United States announced on July 8, 2026, that it will delist Syria as a state sponsor of terrorism, marking a significant shift in its foreign policy. This decision, effective in 45 days unless bl...
